free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of 31.1-MeV protons by
carbon-12
Carbon
A counter telescope consisted of
a proportional counter and a NaI(Tl) scintillation
counter. The proportional counter was filled to 1.5 Atm
with a 91% argon,9% methane mixture. The scintillator
counters consisted of a 1/4-in.-thick NaI(Tl) crystal
optically coupled to an RCA6655A photomultiplier tube.
The differential cross sections for the elastic
scattering have been analyzed using the diffuse-surface
optical model of the nucleus for a wide range of
parameters. The angular distributions of the
inelastically scattered protons are peaked forward and
have been compared to predictions of direct interaction
theories.
Last checking has been done.

the measured angular distributions to zero and 180
degrees, and the quoted errors include the estimate of
the reliability of this extrapolation.
